title: 10 best herbal creams for toothache

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's natural oils, such as menthol and menthone, reduce inflammation and swelling around the affected tooth or gum, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Anesthetic properties: The anesthetic compounds in peppermint, including menthol and cineole, numb the nerve endings, temporarily reducing pain perception and helping to alleviate toothache symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint's antibacterial and antifungal properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the toothache, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint creams numbs the affected area, providing quick relief from toothache pain. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection causing the toothache. The cooling sensation of peppermint creams also helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for toothache relief. Its effectiveness is largely due to its ability to numb and soothe the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Provides a numbing effect, temporarily relieving toothache pain by blocking the nerve signals to the brain.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues that may be causing toothache pain.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the underlying bacterial infection or inflammation that may be contributing to the toothache.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of menthol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Used because they contain menthone, a compound that helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Used for their menthol content, which helps to alleviate toothache pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a well-tended garden in the early morning.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water with 1 cup of dried mentha x piperita leaves in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes over low heat then strain and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Combine the herbal extract with the beeswax mixture and whip until a smooth cream forms.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Sanguisorba minor has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi, which could help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in cases of toothache.

Anti-inflammatory property: The herb contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce pain and swelling associated with toothaches by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.
Analgesic property: Sanguisorba minor has been traditionally used to relieve pain, which could be beneficial in managing toothache symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sanguisorba minor, also known as salad burnet, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Sanguisorba minor have been shown to reduce pain and swelling in the gums, providing relief from toothache. The cream's cooling effect also helps to numb the area, reducing sensitivity and discomfort. Additionally, Sanguisorba minor's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the toothache, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These help by reducing inflammation and pain in the gums and tooth, thereby alleviating toothache symptoms.
Quercetin: This flavonoid helps by acting as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent, reducing pain and swelling associated with toothache.
Rosmarinic acid: This polyphenol helps by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, which in turn reduces pain and inflammation in the tooth and gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth.
Leaves: Employed for their analgesic and antibacterial properties, which aid in soothing toothache pain and preventing infection.
Flowers: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which help calm mouth inflammation and ease toothache discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sanguisorba minor leaves and flowers in late summer when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested sanguisorba minor leaves and flowers using a low-temperature dehydration method.
Steep the dried sanguisorba minor in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain the oil mixture and discard the solids using a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Mix the infused oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax to create a cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that stimulate pain and inflammation in response to tissue damage.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, helping to protect the mouth and teeth from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tooth decay and gum disease.
Triterpenoids: Exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause tooth infection and inflammation, thereby reducing the severity of toothache symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compound glycyrrhizin reduces swelling and pain in the gums and teeth, providing instant relief. Additionally, licorice root extracts have antibacterial properties that help combat infections that may be causing the toothache. The soothing and cooling effects of the cream also help calm the nerves, providing a sense of comfort and relief from the pain. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, specifically glycyrrhizin and licopyranocoumarin, may help alleviate toothache by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that combat bacterial infections and reduce swelling.

Triterpenoids (Glycyrrhizin): Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, may help with toothache by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thus reducing pain and inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce pain and swelling.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in soothing toothache pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce pain and prevent infection in the toothache area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate them in a controlled environment.
Extract 100g of glycyrrhizin from the roots using 1l of 95% ethanol in a 2:1 ratio.
Mix 50g of distilled glycerin with 50g of distilled water to create a humectant solution.
Combine the glycyrrhizin extract with 50g of the humectant solution and 50g of a vegetable emulsifier.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothaches.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of dandelion, including its ability to inhibit bacterial growth, can help combat infections that may be contributing to toothache pain.
Stomachic properties: Dandelion's stomachic properties, which aid digestion and soothe the digestive tract, can also help alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with toothache pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and inulin, have been shown to reduce swelling and kill bacteria that can cause tooth infections. Additionally, the cream's soothing and cooling effects can provide quick relief from toothache pain, making it a natural and effective remedy for temporary toothache relief. The cream's antibacterial properties also help prevent further infection, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with toothache.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause tooth infections and exacerbate toothache.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected tooth and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with toothaches.
Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used for their analgesic and antibacterial properties, which help to numb the pain and prevent infection in the affected area.

Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and surrounding areas of the toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 freshly picked taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves from a clean environment.
Steep 10g of taraxacum officinale in 100ml of carrier oil at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Mix 20g of beeswax, 20g of shea butter, and 10ml of the infused oil with a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a clean container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and salvene, help reduce swelling and pain caused by toothaches.
Antimicrobial: Sage's antimicrobial properties, including thymol and carvacrol, combat bacterial infections that can cause toothaches and promote healing.
Anesthetic: Sage contains compounds like eucalyptol and camphor, which have analgesic and numbing effects, providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in sage, including camphor, borneol, and salvene, work together to reduce swelling and kill bacteria that may be causing the toothache. By applying a sage cream directly to the affected area, you can help alleviate pain and discomfort, providing quick relief from toothache symptoms. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties can help prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound that helps to reduce inflammation and pain in toothache by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Carnosic Acid: A phenolic diterpene that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with toothache.
Ursolic Acid: A triterpenoid compound that has anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain, inflammation, and infection in toothache.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain compounds like carnosic acid and ursolic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate toothache pain.
Roots: They are rich in salvinolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe toothache pain.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves when the plant is in full bloom and the leaves are at their peak potency.
Dry the leaves in a cool dark place for 7 to 14 days to prevent mold and spoilage.
Steep 1 part of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 4 parts of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herb residue and achieve a smooth consistency.
Whip the infused oil with beeswax and a natural emulsifier such as lecithin to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and gums, providing relief from toothaches.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal agents present in aloe vera cream, like salicylic acid and cinnamic acid, prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to tooth decay and infection, helping to alleviate toothache symptoms.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's bioactive compounds, such as gibberellin and polysaccharides, promote collagen synthesis and tissue repair, which can help accelerate the healing process of mouth sores, gum ulcers, or other oral wounds that may be contributing to toothache pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause toothache. Applying aloe vera cream directly to the affected tooth or gum can help soothe the pain and reduce swelling. Its cooling effect can also provide temporary relief from the discomfort associated with toothaches. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate toothache pain.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and pain in the tooth and gum area by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Phenolic compounds (including gallic acid and caffeic acid): These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause toothache, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Tannins: These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the mouth, which in turn helps alleviate toothache symptoms such as pain and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, which is extracted from its leaves, is used to make creams for toothache due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Aloe vera latex, also extracted from its leaves, is used to make creams for toothache due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaf extract, particularly the juice, is used to make creams for toothache due to its antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the aloe vera gel and melted coconut oil mixture in a bowl.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to room temperature.
Pour the herbal aloe vera cream into a jar and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, providing relief from toothache symptoms.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties of pot marigold cream can help to combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting healing.
Wound-healing property: Pot marigold's wound-healing property can help to accelerate the healing process of mouth sores or ulcers associated with toothaches, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. Calendula's ability to combat bacteria and other pathogens also helps to prevent infection, which can exacerbate toothache symptoms. By soothing and protecting the affected area, calendula cream can provide relief and promote healing, making it a natural remedy for toothache sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the mouth, which may contribute to toothache relief.
Terpenes: Possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate pain and prevent infection in the affected tooth or gum area.
Carotenoids: May exhibit antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress and potentially alleviating toothache pain caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce pain and prevent infection.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to numb the affected area and reduce pain, as well as their antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: They are used due to their analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them immediately in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part calendula powder with 4 parts sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until the beeswax melts completely in 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains polyphenols and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth and gums, thus alleviating toothache pain.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in Echinacea may help protect teeth and gums from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of oral infections and tooth decay, which can contribute to toothaches.

Immune system support: Echinacea is known for its ability to boost the immune system, which can help fight off infections that may be causing toothache pain. By supporting the body's natural defenses, Echinacea may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of toothaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections that can cause toothaches. The cream may also provide a numbing effect, providing temporary relief from the pain. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia creams may help to promote healing and reduce inflammation in the gums, making it a potential natural remedy for toothache relief.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, such as echinacoside, may help alleviate toothache by reducing inflammation and pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Alkaloids (specifically, cichoric acid and echinacoside have alkaloid properties): The alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia may help combat toothache by their ability to reduce bacterial growth, which can contribute to the development of tooth infections.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid found in Echinacea angustifolia, may help alleviate toothache by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and acting as an antioxidant to prevent tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are used to make creams for toothache because they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache pain.
Flowers: Echinacea angustifolia flowers are used to make creams for toothache because they contain alkaloids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are used to make creams for toothache because they contain compounds that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of echinacea angustifolia, typically in the fall, after the plant has completed its life cycle.
Dry the harvested roots, either by air-drying or using a dehydrator, at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, in a ratio of 1:5 with a carrier oil.
Infuse the powdered echinacea in a carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the infused oil, filter it through a cheesecloth, and mix it with beeswax to create a stable cream, using a ratio of 1:1.

remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Lemon balm cream's antiseptic property helps to reduce bacteria and prevent infection, which can contribute to toothaches by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort.
Analgesic: Lemon balm's analgesic property, which comes from its flavonoid content, helps to numb the pain of a toothache, providing temporary relief without numbing the entire mouth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and linalool, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth and gums. By applying a Melissa officinalis cream to the affected area, it can help to numb the pain and soothe the discomfort associated with a toothache, providing relief and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate toothache pain and reduce swelling.
Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, caryophyllene may help numb the pain associated with toothaches.
Geranial: This monoterpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the underlying bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate toothache pain.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause toothache.
Flowers: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers in early morning after dew has evaporated to prevent water contamination.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 6 hours or air dry them for 7 days.
Infuse 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 24 hours of infusion time.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:1:0.1 by weight and heat the mixture until the beeswax melts.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help reduce swelling and pain in the gum tissue, providing relief from toothache symptoms.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of wild marjoram cream, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, can help combat underlying infections that may be causing the toothache.
Anesthetic property: Wild marjoram's anesthetic compounds, such as bornyl acetate, have been shown to numb the affected area, reducing sensitivity and pain associated with a toothache.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare, also known as wild marjoram, creams helps with toothache because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Origanum vulgare, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to reduce swelling and combat the underlying bacterial infections that often cause toothache pain. Additionally, the analgesic and antiseptic properties of the cream help to numb the pain and promote healing, providing quick relief from toothache discomfort. This makes Origanum vulgare creams a popular natural remedy for toothache relief.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with toothaches.
Carvacrol: A phenolic compound with antibacterial properties, carvacrol can help combat infection and prevent further inflammation, reducing the severity of toothache pain.
Linalool: A terpene with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, linalool may help numb the pain and reduce swelling, providing temporary relief from toothache discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate toothache pain.
Stems: The stems of Origanum vulgare contain essential oils that have analgesic and antibacterial properties, making them useful for toothache relief.
Seeds: Origanum vulgare seeds contain compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves and flowers when in full bloom and at peak potency.
Infuse the plant material in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for several weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter to create a cream base.
Pour the cream base into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with toothache because of its natural anesthetic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains compounds like estragole, fenchone, and anethole that work together to numb the affected area, providing instant relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's antiseptic properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, promoting a faster healing process. By applying the cream directly to the affected tooth or gum, individuals can experience rapid alleviation of toothache symptoms, allowing them to go about their daily routine without interruption.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Fennel's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to toothache pain.
Analgesic property: Fennel's analgesic compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with toothaches.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth tissues, which can exacerbate toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in essential oils and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing toothache pain and inflammation.
Barks: Fennel barks contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help to reduce bacterial growth and alleviate tooth sensitivity.
remedy_recipe: Collect 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and crush them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed foeniculum vulgare powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at room temperature for one hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil to the reserved infused oil, melting them together in the double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for application as a topical cream, taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that inhibits the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. When applied topically to the affected area, St John's Wort cream can help numb the pain and reduce swelling around the tooth, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, its antibacterial properties may also help combat underlying infections that could be contributing to the toothache.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort contains hyperforin, which helps to reduce inflammation in the mouth, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with toothaches.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria and viruses, helping to combat infections that may be contributing to toothache symptoms.
Anxiolytic (stress-relieving) properties: St John's Wort has been traditionally used to calm nerves and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to toothache-related tension and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds like hypericin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing toothache pain.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and antimicrobial effects, helping to alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and dry them thoroughly.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 30 milliliters of jojoba oil with 5 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler and heat for 20 minutes.
Add 10 grams of the powdered hypericum perforatum to the oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool before pouring into containers and allowing to set for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with toothache because of its natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The lemongrass oil present in the cream has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are responsible for causing inflammation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to alleviate tooth sensitivity and discomfort, providing relief from toothaches. As a result, using herbal lemongrass creams can be an effective natural remedy for temporary toothache relief.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lemongrass oil has antimicrobial properties that can help reduce bacteria and other microorganisms that may be causing or contributing to a toothache.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with toothaches.
Anesthetic property: The citral content in lemongrass oil has been shown to have anesthetic properties, which can help numb the affected area and provide relief from pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with toothaches.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and antibacterial properties, helping to alleviate toothache pain and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5 drops of tea tree essential oil and stir well to combine, allowing the mixture to infuse for 24 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and allowing it to cool to room temperature.
Whip 50g of beeswax with 100ml of the cymbopogon citratus-infused coconut oil in a separate double boiler, heating it gently over low heat for 10 minutes until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with toothache because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-ionone and geraniol, work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and mouth tissues, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate tooth infections. As a result, Damask rose creams provide natural relief from toothache discomfort, allowing for a sense of calm and renewed oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Rose cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce swelling and pain in the gum and surrounding tissues, providing relief from toothache.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial activity of rose extracts may help combat bacterial infections that cause tooth decay, abscesses, or gum disease, reducing the severity of toothache symptoms.
Anodyne property: Rose oil's anodyne (pain-relieving) properties can numb the affected area, providing immediate relief from toothache pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of salicylic acid, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain caused by toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena petals in early morning for maximum oil content and fragrance retention.
Dry the harvested petals in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Infuse 10g of dried rose petals in 100ml of sweet almond oil by mixing and storing at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 50% of the infused oil with beeswax, 20% with coconut oil, and add 10 drops of peppermint essential oil, stirring until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and chamazulene, can penetrate through the gums to reduce swelling and ease pain caused by dental issues. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects help to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension, making it easier to manage toothache discomfort. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective way to alleviate toothache symptoms without harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, making it easier to manage discomfort and promoting faster healing.

Antibacterial properties: Chamomile's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause tooth infections and abscesses, helping to prevent further infection and promote wound closure.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic effects help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with tooth pain, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve toothache pain.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that can reduce inflammation and soothe the gums, making them effective for oral applications.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground flowers with 100 ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining 70 grams of the liquid extract.
Combine the liquid extract with 10 grams of beeswax and heat gently until melted, stirring occasionally, taking approximately 15 minutes to melt completely.
Pour the warm mixture into clean sterilized tubes or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature, resulting in a creamy consistency within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with toothache because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and mouth. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that cause inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing toothaches. Additionally, turmeric's antibacterial properties help combat infections that can lead to tooth decay and gum disease, further alleviating symptoms of a toothache. By applying a topical cream containing turmeric, patients can find relief from discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches by reducing inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of turmeric cream can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the toothache, thereby promoting healing and reducing the risk of further infection.
Anesthetic: Some herbal turmeric creams contain ingredients like clove oil or wintergreen oil, which have analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and teeth.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in antioxidants and have natural analgesic properties that can help numb the pain of toothache and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried turmeric root powder and coconut oil.
Mix one teaspoon of turmeric powder with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until smooth consistency.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 24 hours before use.
Store the curcuma longa cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise creams helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil extracted from anise seeds, also known as Pimpinella anisum, contains compounds that numb the pain and reduce swelling in the affected area. When applied topically to the teeth and gums, these creams provide rapid relief from toothaches by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain, thus providing a soothing and comforting sensation.
remedy_properties: Anethole content: Anethole, a compound found in anise oil, has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial properties of anise oil help to combat bacterial infections that can cause tooth decay and abscesses, reducing the risk of spreading infection and alleviating symptoms of toothache.
Soothing properties: Anise creams often contain soothing herbs like chamomile or lavender, which calm the mouth and gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort caused by a toothache.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the anise plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Buds: The buds of the anise plant contain compounds that help to numb the gums and teeth, providing relief from toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried pimpinella anisum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered pimpinella anisum with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 5 minutes.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the mixture until fully incorporated.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cooled cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 30 days or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with toothache because they contain isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain. The cream's antibacterial properties also combat bacteria responsible for tooth decay and gum disease, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, red clover's flavonoids inhibit platelet aggregation, reducing blood clotting and inflammation around the affected area, providing relief from toothache discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Antimicrobial property: Red clover cream has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to the toothache.
Analgesic property: The isoflavones in red clover cream have been shown to have analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with toothaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with toothaches.
Buds: Contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried trifolium pratense flowers and carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Combine one tablespoon of dried trifolium pratense flowers with three tablespoons of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and maceration.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with three tablespoons of the trifolium pratense oil and heat gently over low heat until fully incorporated and cooled to form a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with toothache because of its natural antibacterial properties that combat the underlying infection causing the pain. The thyme oil in these creams is able to penetrate through the gum tissue and into the infected area, helping to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. Additionally, thyme's analgesic properties help to numb the affected area, providing instant relief from toothache discomfort. As a result, applying herbal thyme creams topically can provide fast and effective pain relief for individuals suffering from toothaches.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause tooth infections and abscesses, reducing inflammation and pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches by decreasing inflammation and irritation in the mouth.
Anesthetic properties: The aromatic compounds found in thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain, making them effective in alleviating toothache symptoms.
Buds: Thyme buds are rich in essential oils that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infection and relieve toothache discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them properly for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried thyme leaves with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six hours or overnight allowing the thyme oils to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix one teaspoon of the extracted thymol-rich oil with two teaspoons of beeswax and one teaspoon of coconut oil melting them together gently over low heat then whipping until smooth.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for toothache are topical ointments that contain natural ingredients to help alleviate toothache pain and inflammation. These creams work by reducing swelling and numbing the affected area, providing temporary relief from toothache discomfort. Some of the best herbal creams for toothache include Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Syzygium aromaticum, Mentha x piperita, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Salvia officinalis, Cinchona officinalis, and Calendula officinalis. If left untreated, toothaches can lead to sleepless nights, decreased productivity, and even anxiety, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal creams are muscle and joint pains, such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and tennis elbow. These creams often contain ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and menthol, which help reduce inflammation, ease tension, and numb the affected area. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to alleviate headaches, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing without harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with toothache is a mixture of clove oil-infused cream, tea tree oil cream, and eucalyptus oil cream. Clove oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and swelling. Tea tree oil's antibacterial properties fight infection, while eucalyptus oil's cooling effect numbs the area. Apply a small amount of each cream to the affected tooth or gum, gently massaging until absorbed. This blend can provide quick relief from toothache discomfort and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in Zingiber officinale, has natural pain-relieving effects that target the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for toothache pain. Additionally, the cream's warming sensation helps to reduce swelling and ease pressure on the affected area. By applying the cream directly to the gums, it can provide fast and effective relief from toothache discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for toothache sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the gums and teeth.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving toothache pain and inflammation.
Zingibain: As a proteolytic enzyme, zingibain helps break down protein-based compounds that may contribute to toothache pain, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots are used as they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothache.
Barks: Barks are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning after the dew has dried up for 1 hour.
Dry the harvested zingiber officinale rhizomes in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 250 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture with 50 grams of shea butter and 5 drops of vitamin e oil using an electric mixer for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in Eucalyptus globulus acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected area and providing temporary relief from pain. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the toothache. The cooling sensation of the cream also helps to soothe the gums and provide comfort, making it a popular natural remedy for toothache relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This constituent helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
α-Pinene: This terpene constituent helps with toothache by acting as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected area and providing relief from pain.
Caryophyllene oxide: This terpenoid constituent helps with toothache by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing swelling and preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help alleviate toothache pain.
Barks: The bark's eucalyptol content also contributes to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects in toothache creams.
Roots: The roots' bioactive compounds, including eucalyptol and other terpenoids, help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and clean them thoroughly under running cold water for 5 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of eucalyptus globulus leaves with 2 cups of distilled water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter together in a small saucepan over low heat until melted.
Combine the cooled liquid from step 3 with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and stir until well combined.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum creams helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, eugenol, found in the Syzygium aromaticum plant, has natural pain-relieving and antibacterial properties that help reduce swelling and ease toothache pain. When applied topically to the affected area, the cream helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from toothache discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate toothache pain and promote oral health.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: It helps with toothache by acting as a local anesthetic and analgesic, reducing pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Caryophyllene oxide: It contributes to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infection and reduce swelling associated with toothache.
Linalool: It possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by toothache.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate toothache pain.
Seeds: They are used because of their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which aid in preventing infections in toothache conditions.
Barks: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight infections in toothache conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather syzygium aromaticum essential oil 10 drops, beeswax 2 tablespoons, coconut oil 2 tablespoons, and shea butter 2 tablespoons.
Combine coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler to melt at 180-190°f for 10 minutes.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool to 120-140°f for 10 minutes.
Add syzygium aromaticum essential oil to the mixture and stir well to incorporate for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in peppermint oil, such as menthol and menthone, work to numb the affected area and reduce pain. When applied topically to the affected tooth or gum, the cream can help to ease discomfort and reduce swelling. Additionally, peppermint oil's natural antibacterial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the toothache. This makes Mentha x piperita creams a popular natural remedy for tooth pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with toothache by numbing the affected area, reducing pain perception, and providing temporary relief.
Menthone: Contributes to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of the herb, helping to alleviate toothache pain and reduce swelling.
Limonene: Exhibits local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to numb the affected tooth and surrounding tissues, providing relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for toothache due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used due to their ability to provide a soothing and calming effect when applied topically.
Rhyzomes: Used for their antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and ease toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh m x piperita leaves and flowers when they are at their peak fragrance and potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material for 4 hours to obtain 50ml of essential oil.
Weigh out 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Combine 5ml of essential oil with 20g of aloe vera gel and add it to the cooled mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with toothache because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil found in these creams has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain by targeting the source of the infection. Its analgesic properties also help to numb the area, providing relief from the discomfort associated with toothaches. Additionally, tea tree oil has been found to have antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent further infection and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps with toothache by acting as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent, numbing the pain and reducing swelling in the affected area.
Caldendrin: It helps with toothache by exerting antimicrobial properties, reducing bacterial growth and infection that may be contributing to the pain.
Limonene: It helps with toothache by acting as a solvent and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high content of cineole, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache pain.
Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are often used to create creams that can help soothe and numb toothache pain.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain essential oils that can be extracted and used to create creams for toothache relief, often due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers in the early morning when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Steam distill the plant material for 3 hours to obtain 20ml of essential oil.
Melt 100g of candelilla wax in a double boiler at 180-190°f for 30 minutes.
Mix the essential oil with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens into a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected tooth and surrounding gums. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's ability to combat bacterial and fungal infections can help prevent the spread of infection, promoting a faster recovery. Isoquinoline alkaloids: Help alleviate toothache by possessing analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which work together to provide pain relief and reduce swelling in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Echinacea purpurea due to their higher concentration of bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Flowers: The flowers are used for their high content of alkaloids, such as echinacoside, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for at least 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea with 1 part beeswax in a double boiler to melt.
Add 2 tablespoons of sweet almond oil and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil to the melted mixture.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and pour it into small containers for cooling and solidification.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum creams helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream contains cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth. When applied topically, it numbs the affected area, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with toothaches. Additionally, Cinnamomum zeylanicum's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection that may be causing the toothache, promoting faster healing and recovery.

Eugenol: Eugenol has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help numb the pain and reduce swelling in the gums and teeth, providing relief from toothache.
Cinnamic acid: Cinnamic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and teeth, making it an effective constituent in addressing toothache.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems are used to make creams for toothache because they contain cinnamaldehyde, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: The barks of Cinnamomum zeylanicum are used to make creams for toothache due to their high content of cinnamaldehyde, which helps in reducing pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for toothache because they contain various oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Melt coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 10 grams of cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oil to the melted coconut oil.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax with the coconut oil mixture in a bowl.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 5 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into glass jars and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Salvia, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the mouth. Additionally, the cream's ability to combat bacteria and other microorganisms may help to prevent infection and promote healing in the affected tooth or gum. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from toothache pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help with toothache by reducing inflammation and astringent properties that combat bacterial infections in the mouth.
Rosmarinic acid: This compound assists in toothache relief by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent that reduces pain and swelling.
Salvianolic acid: This constituent helps alleviate toothache by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that reduce pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe toothache pain.
Barks: They are used for their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Extract the salvia officinalis leaves by steeping them in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the leaves.
Mix the infused oil with a base ingredient like beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler to melt and combine.
Add a preservative like vitamin e oil and essential oil of rose to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The key compound, quinine, found in Cinchona officinalis, has a long history of use in treating various pain conditions, including toothache. When applied topically to the affected area, Cinchona officinalis creams can reduce inflammation and numb the pain, providing relief from toothache. The cream's soothing properties also help to calm the gums and surrounding tissues, promoting a more comfortable oral environment.

Quinovic acid glycosides: Quinovic acid glycosides have antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce bacterial growth and prevent further infection, contributing to the healing process of the toothache.
Quinic acid: Quinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected tooth and surrounding area, providing relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make creams for toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Used to make creams for toothache due to its local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Used to make creams for toothache as it contains compounds that provide relief from pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinchona officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal quality and potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to remove excess moisture.
Chop the dried cinchona officinalis leaves into small pieces to enhance their surface area for infusion.
Steep the chopped cinchona officinalis leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 weeks to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion from the carrier oil and mix it with a thickening agent such as beeswax and shea butter to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Additionally, the cream's antimicrobial properties can help to combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache. This natural remedy can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with toothaches, making it a popular alternative to traditional painkillers. Regular application can also promote healing and prevent further infections.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and teeth.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache.
Sesquiterpenes: These constituents have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used in creams due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also used in creams for their soothing and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate toothache symptoms.
Seeds: Although less common, seeds are sometimes used in creams for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers and 10 grams of jojoba oil in a mixing bowl.
Infuse the calendula flowers in 100 milliliters of jojoba oil for four hours in a double boiler.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 80 milliliters of liquid.
Combine the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 180 degrees fahrenheit.
